  "reasoning_paragraph": "E7 activates on the mutual fund dimension of case c-2026-05-21-malhotra-01, with scope limited to evaluating the existing MF allocation and the MF-side consequences of the proposed reallocation; the proposed target instrument (Stallion Asset Core Fund, PMS) falls outside E7 jurisdiction and is referred to E6. Of the four existing MF schemes, only Mirae Asset Large Cap Fund carries sufficient snapshot data for a scheme-level verdict: the fund presents a well-documented quality-tilted large-cap mandate with 15+ years of lead manager continuity under Neelesh Surana, active share of approximately 75%, sustained alpha of 210-240 basis points over 3Y and 5Y horizons across multiple market regimes, a competitive Direct plan TER of 0.95%, and no capacity stress at Rs 30,000 Cr AUM given the large-cap mandate's structural depth; on these dimensions the scheme warrants a positive scheme-level assessment. The proposed Rs 50 L partial trim of this position to fund a first-time PMS allocation introduces two material MF-side concerns: first, it reduces a position where manager skill is documented and multi-regime alpha is attributable to stock selection rather than beta drift, in favour of a vehicle where no comparable snapshot data exists to confirm equivalent risk-adjusted return characteristics; second, the trim crystallises a taxable event under the post-Budget 2024 capital gains regime (LTCG at 12.5% on equity MF gains beyond Rs 1.25 L annual threshold, effective 2024-07-23), creating a real return drag on effective proceeds deployed into the PMS. The SIP redirect component of the funding plan affects existing MF accumulation but the specific schemes targeted for reduction are unspecified, precluding scheme-level impact assessment.
